---
title: About this site
---

The Hagströmer Library is part of Karolinska Institutet and consists of over
150,000 books and journals connected to disciplines adjacent to the medical
sciences. The main collection derives from Karolinska Institutet and the
Swedish Medical Association (Svenska Läkareförbundet) and the collection
specialises in material from the period 1600-1990. The mission of the library
is to preserve the cultural heritage of Karolinska Institutet, and it is the
core of the Medical History and Heritage unit (MHK) at Karolinska Institutet.

As a part of the [_Secrets to Patent_](https://secretstopatents.org/) project,
we have digitised and processed the catalogue cards for the library's main
collection. Each card has been extracted from scanned volume pages, processed
with OCR, and post-processed with to perform som basic cleaning and correction,
such as extracting the top-corner of each card to make sure we got the
catalogue code. All the original PDF-files are available on ALVIN, where they
are published under a CC-00 license.

The digitization and processing and hosting of the catalogue has been done by
Mathias Johansson, Cecilia Lundström, and Natacha Klein
Käfer using Python, the VLM `qwen3-vl:2b`, Ollama, Quarto, GitHub Pages and Claude.
Small random samples of the OCR output have been manually reviewed.

## License

[![CC BY-NC 4.0](https://licensebuttons.net/l/by-nc/4.0/88x31.png)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/)

This work is licensed under a
[Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/)
(CC BY-NC 4.0).

## Contact

For questions or feedback, contact Mathias Johansson at
[MathiasJohansson@kultur.lu.se](mailto:MathiasJohansson@kultur.lu.se).
